summary: Parallel review: FAIL (3 critical, 15 warning, 15 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: 🟡 bin/lib/gate.mjs:19 — `taskId` passed directly into `path.join` at line 112 without validation; crafted value `../../tmp/evil` writes artifacts outside `.team`; add `/^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$/` guard after `getFlag(args, "task")` (carried from Security run_4; confirmed present in current code)

🟡 bin/lib/validator-parsers.mjs:49 — `parseJunitXml` pushes `message`/`classname`/`file` verbatim without control-char stripping; `parseTap:81` and `parseGithubActions:118` both sanitize; inconsistent security posture across three parsers sharing `meta.messages` (carried from Security task-1 run_1; confirmed present)

The core requirement is met end-to-end: `::error file=src/foo.js,line=5::message` produces `findings.critical === 1` and forces `verdict: "FAIL"` through the full `gate.mjs` production path, even when the validator exits 0. Two 🟡 backlog items remain; neither blocks the stated requirement. Eval written to `tasks/task-3/eval.md`.

🟡 `bin/lib/validator-parsers.mjs:117` — Greedy `(.*)` splits at LAST `::` in the line; if the message body itself contains `::` (e.g. `::error file=src/foo.js,line=5::TypeError: unexpected :: token`), `m[2]` absorbs part of the message as properties, corrupting `props.line` and the stored location string; `findings.critical` is still incremented correctly but the stored label is wrong; empirically confirmed; add a test covering this case

🟡 `bin/lib/validator-parsers.mjs:49` — `parseJunitXml` still pushes attacker-controlled `message`, `classname`, and `file` verbatim without control-char stripping; `parseTap:81` and `parseGithubActions:120` both sanitize — inconsistent security posture across three parsers sharing the same `meta.messages` interface (carried from Security/Architect task-1 run_1)

The core requirement — `::error file=src/foo.js,line=5::message` → one critical finding — is met, tested across 25 passing tests, and the gate enforcement is wired end-to-end. Two backlog 🟡 items: the `::` in message body corruption, and the JUnit control-char gap.

🟡 `bin/lib/gate.mjs:19` — `taskId` used in `path.join` at line 111 with no character validation; a crafted value `../../tmp/evil` causes `mkdirSync`+`writeFileSync` to write outside `.team`; validate `taskId` with `/^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$/` before use
🟡 `bin/lib/gate.mjs:60` — `execSync` called without explicit `maxBuffer`; Node.js default is version-dependent; add `maxBuffer: 10 * 1024 * 1024`

🟡 `.team/features/external-validator-integration/tasks/task-3/handshake.json:7` — claims "27 total tests, all passing"; direct count of `it()` blocks is 25 (6+6+3+10); 2-count discrepancy in handshake evidence

🟡 `bin/lib/validator-parsers.mjs:49` — `parseJunitXml` pushes attacker-controlled `message`/`classname`/`file` verbatim without control-char stripping; `parseTap:81` and `parseGithubActions:118` both sanitize — inconsistent security posture across parsers sharing the same interface

🟡 `test/integration.test.mjs` — no integration test for `--output-format github-actions`; `gate.mjs:101` (the `critical > 0 → FAIL` verdict logic) and `gate.mjs:106–107` (parser dispatch) are exercised in production but not tested; a regression to exit-code-only verdict passes all tests undetected; add cmdGate test: command echoes `::error file=src/foo.js,line=5::lint error`, exits 0, `--output-format github-actions`, assert `verdict === "FAIL"` and `handshake.findings.critical === 1`

🟡 `test/validator-parsers.test.mjs:230` — `::notice` test asserts `findings.suggestion === 1` only; removing `messages.push(label)` in the notice branch at `validator-parsers.mjs:149` passes all tests undetected; add `assert.equal(result.meta.messages.length, 1)`

The two 🟡 items go to backlog. The integration test gap is the higher-priority one: no test exercises the complete `exit-0 + ::error → FAIL` path, which is the primary behavioral promise of this feature.

🔴 `bin/lib/gate.mjs:19,111` — `taskId` from `--task` CLI flag used in `path.join()` without character validation; `taskId="../../etc"` resolves outside `.team`; an AI agent controls both the write destination (via `--task`) and the content (via `--cmd`), yielding an arbitrary file-write primitive to any path writable by the process; validate with `/^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$/` after `getFlag(args, "task")` at line 19 and reject if it fails

🟡 `bin/lib/validator-parsers.mjs:49` — `parseJunitXml` pushes `message`, `classname`, and `file` from attacker-influenced XML verbatim to `meta.messages[]` without control-char stripping; `parseTap:81` and `parseGithubActions:120` both sanitize; add `replace(/[\x00-\x1f\x7f]/g, '')` before `messages.push()` in `parseJunitXml` (carried from task-1 Security run_1)
🟡 `bin/lib/gate.mjs:60` — `execSync` invoked without `maxBuffer` option; Node.js default is version-dependent; add `maxBuffer: 10 * 1024 * 1024` (carried from Security run_4)

The core acceptance criterion is met and well-tested. Four prior findings are confirmed resolved in the current code: the regex colon-handling bug (now greedy `(.*)` splitting at last `::`), the gate dead code, the verdict ignoring parsed findings (now `exitCode === 0 && parsed.findings.critical === 0`), and the control-char test coverage. One 🔴 blocks merge: `taskId` path traversal at `gate.mjs:111` gives a manipulated AI agent an arbitrary file-write primitive via crafted `--task` + `--cmd` arguments. Fix is a one-line validation guard.

🔴 bin/lib/validator-parsers.mjs:174 — `export const PARSERS` has no external consumer (confirmed by grep); `getParser` is the sole intended public API; exporting the registry directly is speculative extensibility with no stated requirement (gold-plating); fix: remove `export` keyword — one word, zero functional change
